zotero.abstractNote: Neural circuits are often characterized by a high degree of degeneracy: many different combinations of parameters can produce similar circuit dynamics. This degeneracy poses a challenge for understanding the relationship between circuit structure and function. Here, we introduce a framework based on deep inverse modeling to identify the manifold of parameters that are consistent with observed neural dynamics. We show that this approach can recover the underlying parameter-to-dynamics mapping and identify dynamic-dependent invariances in neural circuit mechanisms. We demonstrate our approach on a range of neural circuit models, including a conductance-based model of a single neuron and a recurrent neural network model of a population of neurons. Our results suggest that deep inverse modeling is a powerful tool for uncovering the principles of neural circuit organization and for understanding how circuit structure constrains neural dynamics.
